title: BattleDebug Finding
description: A finding from the BattleDebug agent combat testing system

id.description: Unique finding ID in format F-YYYY-MM-DD-NNN

experiment.description: Name of the experiment that produced this evidence

n_battles.description: Number of battles in the experiment

raw_data_path.description: Path to the full experiment results file

hypothesis.description: Theory about the root cause of the issue

suggested_action.description: Recommended next step or fix approach

code_paths.description: Likely code locations to investigate (file:line format)

updated_at.description: When the finding was last updated

examples.title: Halberdier anti-cav bonus survives flanking
examples.summary: When flanked by cavalry, halberdiers retain their full SPEAR_VS_CAVALRY_BONUS (1.25x). This makes spear units overpowered against cavalry regardless of positioning, eliminating tactical counterplay.
examples.hypothesis: The disorder_matchup_mult is applied AFTER counter_matchup is already baked into the damage calculation in melee_resolver.gd, so the spear bonus is never reduced by flanking disorder.
examples.suggested_action: Verify that counter_matchup multiplier is applied multiplicatively with disorder modifier, not additively. Check melee_resolver.gd around line 256.

examples.title: HARD difficulty only 8% harder than NORMAL
examples.summary: Win rate gap between NORMAL (48%) and HARD (44%) is only 8%, well below the expected 15% gap. This makes difficulty progression feel flat.
examples.hypothesis: The ai_damage_dealt_mult for HARD (1.15) is too conservative. The AI aggression and flanking skill increases don't compensate.
examples.suggested_action: Increase HARD ai_damage_dealt_mult from 1.15 to 1.25, or increase ai_aggression_mult from 1.2 to 1.35

examples.title: Cavalry charges feel weak against infantry
examples.summary: Despite correct charge mechanics, player feedback indicates cavalry charges 'feel' ineffective against infantry. The charge impact damage (mass * speed * 2 / 3 = ~6-8 casualties) may be visually underwhelming.
examples.hypothesis: The charge impact casualties are spread over a single frame without distinctive visual/audio feedback, making them blend with normal combat. The impact should be more visually distinct.
examples.suggested_action: Consider adding screen shake, distinct hit sound, or brief slow-motion on charge impact to make it feel more impactful
